name: Bahrain
summary: Day 49. Iraqi militia drone attacks continue DESPITE ceasefire — Iran has NOT ordered proxy ceasefire. Named as one of 5 Arab states Iran demands reparations from (REPORTED). Houthis specifically warned Bahrain about Bab el-Mandeb. IMF cut 2026 MENA growth to 1.1% from 3.9% — Bahrain hit through financial services/energy disruption. Cumulative damage envelope: 5th Fleet HQ, Bapco, ALBA, Batelco/AWS, Causeway. King Fahd Causeway still closed. Bahrain has NOT made high-level Iran outreach publicly; UAE Deputy PM Mansour called Ghalibaf April 15 (first high-level Gulf-Iran contact). Day 4 of blockade: no Iranian kinetic action against Bahrain-bound vessels despite Iran's April 13 counter-blockade announcement (declaratory only).

signals.content: Iranian strikes targeted US Navy 5th Fleet HQ in Manama and Crowne Plaza Hotel.
Bahrain's Bapco declared force majeure after waves of Iranian strikes targeted energy installations. 3 people killed.

Air raid sirens activated across Bahrain early Sunday March 29. Bahrain Interior Ministry urged citizens to take shelter and 'remain calm.' Simultaneous sirens across Kuwait, Bahrain, and Israel — broadest regional bombardment pattern of the conflict. Consistent with CSIS assessment that Iran has abandoned calibrated retaliation for 'unbridled escalation.' Bushehr nuclear plant (across the Persian Gulf from Bahrain) struck for third time in 10 days — Kuwait issued warning about potential radioactive leak risk from Bushehr. (Sources: Middle East Eye, Bahrain Interior Ministry, CNN)

IRGC struck ALBA aluminum plant (world's largest smelter) — 2 mildly injured, damage assessed. 20 missiles and 23 UAVs intercepted March 28, with damage to Bapco energy facilities. ALBA already shut 3 of 6 smelting lines (19% capacity) due to Hormuz disruption. New targeting category: industrial infrastructure with alleged US military supply chain links. (Sources: CNBC, JPost, The National, Al Arabiya, Al Jazeera)

IRGC missiles hit Batelco telecommunications facility in Hamala, Bahrain on April 1 — the facility hosts Amazon Web Services infrastructure. AWS servers rendered 'partly inoperative.' Bahrain Interior Ministry confirmed civil defense teams responded to fire at the site. This is NOT the first AWS strike — Iran previously hit AWS data centers in UAE and Bahrain in early March — but is the first strike under the new '18 companies' threat framework announced by IRGC. Targeting doctrine continues expanding: military -> energy -> industrial -> telecom/cloud infrastructure. CONFIRMED. (Sources: Free Press Journal, Gizmodo, Daily Sabah, Bahrain Interior Ministry)

IRGC struck Batelco telecommunications facility in Hamala, Bahrain on April 1 — hosts AWS cloud infrastructure, servers rendered 'partly inoperative.' Bahrain Interior Ministry confirmed fire at facility. This was the SINGLE confirmed strike from the IRGC's 18-company corporate deadline threat. Also, ALBA aluminum smelter damage from March 29 strike ongoing — 19% capacity still shut. Bahrain taking sustained hits: ALBA (March 29), Batelco/AWS (April 1). (Sources: Free Press Journal, Gizmodo, Daily Sabah, Bahrain Interior Ministry)

CONFIRMED: Bahrain's Bapco Energies oil storage tank hit by Iranian drone attack on April 5. Fire extinguished, no injuries, damage being assessed. This is the second confirmed strike on Bahrain energy infrastructure (first was Batelco telecom/AWS facility April 1-2). Iran now targeting energy infrastructure across FOUR Gulf states simultaneously: Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE, Qatar. (Sources: Gulf News, Xinhua, The National, GDN Online Bahrain)

No new strikes reported April 6 as of morning assessment. Bapco oil storage fire from April 5 extinguished. Damage assessment ongoing. ALBA 19% capacity still shut. Bahrain has absorbed sustained multi-vector attacks: 5th Fleet HQ (Feb 28), Bapco energy (Mar 9, Apr 5), ALBA aluminum (Mar 29), Batelco/AWS telecom (Apr 1). Targeting pattern covers military, energy, industrial, and telecom infrastructure.

Day 8+ of consecutive Gulf infrastructure attacks. The geography of the war is now uniformly hostile to Gulf energy: UAE (Borouge), Bahrain (Bapco), Kuwait (Mina, Shuwaikh, Al Ahmadi + Ali Al Salem 15 US wounded), Saudi (Eastern Province 7 BMs intercepted with debris near energy facilities). Bahrain remains within the cumulative damage envelope. Bapco strike effects continue.

EVENING UPDATE: King Fahd Causeway (25km bridge connecting Bahrain to Saudi Arabia) closed indefinitely due to Iranian attack threats. Combined with Bapco refinery total loss, Bahrain increasingly isolated. Saudi overland resupply route cut. (Sources: Haaretz)

Bahrain intercepted 6 Iranian missiles and 31 drones within hours of ceasefire taking effect on April 8. Bahrain continues to be targeted despite hosting US Fifth Fleet/NAVCENT. (Source: CNBC, Al Jazeera)

Bahrain intercepted seven drones from Iranian-backed Iraqi militias and summoned Iraq's charge d'affaires (CONFIRMED — ISW, The National). Drone attacks continue DESPITE ceasefire — demonstrating Iran has NOT ordered its proxy partners to cease attacks on regional states even while the Iran-US ceasefire formally holds. Named as one of 5 Arab states from which Iran demands reparations for facilitating US-Israeli strikes (REPORTED — Middle East Eye, The National). Houthis specifically warned Bahrain about potential Bab el-Mandeb closure (source: JPost). The simultaneity of negotiate + proxy attacks continue is itself the signal per project methodology.

Day 4 of US blockade / Iran counter-blockade: NO confirmed Iranian kinetic action against Bahrain-bound vessels. Iraqi militia drone attacks on Bahrain continue despite ceasefire (pattern established). IMF cut 2026 MENA growth forecast to 1.1% from 3.9% — Bahrain affected through energy infrastructure disruption (Bapco) and regional financial services. Bahrain has NOT made high-level Iran outreach publicly; UAE Deputy PM Mansour called Ghalibaf April 15 (FIRST high-level Gulf-Iran contact). Bahrain lagging UAE on Gulf-Iran de-escalation. (Sources: IMF WEO, Al Jazeera, CNBC, NBC News live blog)

edges.context: Day 47. Iraqi militia drones intercepted (7) despite ceasefire — Iran NOT ordering proxy ceasefire. Named as reparations target. Houthis warned specifically. Cumulative: Bapco, ALBA, Batelco/AWS, Causeway. 3 killed earlier.
US Navy 5th Fleet HQ in Manama. Critical for US naval operations in Gulf. AWS infrastructure also targeted — US tech companies now in crosshairs.
